mnemonics.title: Phong Components Memory
mnemonics.content: Remember Phong model as 'A.D.S' = Ambient + Diffuse + Specular. Think 'All Day Sunshine' to recall the three components.

mnemonics.title: Shading Methods Order
mnemonics.content: Remember shading quality order: 'FGP' = Flat (worst) → Gouraud → Phong (best). Also: 'FGP' = Faceted → Gradual → Per-pixel.

mnemonics.title: Lambert's Law
mnemonics.content: Lambert = 'Light Angle' - More directly facing light = More brightness. Think 'LAMBERT = Light Angle Brings Equal Rotation to' - angle matters!

acronyms.usage: Describes how surfaces reflect light in different viewing directions; fundamental to realistic rendering
Three-component illumination model for realistic specular highlights on surfaces
Lambert term calculating cosine of angle between surface normal and light direction

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a sphere with a light above it. Gouraud shading shows smooth color gradient but the white highlight is blurred. Phong shading shows a sharp, crisp white highlight because normals are interpolated per pixel.

Think of a billiards ball under a spotlight. The bright white spot (specular highlight) appears where the reflection of light enters your eye. Move your head - the spot moves because it depends on view direction (R·V).
Think of being in a room with no windows - there's still some light everywhere (ambient). It comes from all directions equally, no shadows, just a base brightness preventing complete blackness.

analogies.analogy: Like the background noise in a room - even when no one is speaking directly to you, there's always some base level of light/energy present from indirect sources.
Like matte paper - shine a flashlight on it, the light scatters equally in all directions. The brightness depends only on angle, not where you view it from.
Like a shiny mirror or polished car - the bright highlight you see is the reflection of the light source itself. It depends on both light angle AND where you're standing.

analogies.analization: Think of painting by numbers vs painting every pixel individually. Flat shading = one color per shape. Gouraud = color at corners interpolated. Phong = decide color at every single point.
